content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 867 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660         322 5660           322 5601          327 5441           322 6444

March 12, 1980


Honorable Rose Elizabeth Bird
Chief Justice
Supreme Court of California
State Building
San Francisco, CA  94102

Dear Chief Justice Bird:

     Tom Houston has asked me to respond to your letter to
him of February 21, 1980, requesting this agency's inter-
pretation of Government Code Sections 87202 and 87205 of the
Political Reform Act.1/

     First, you have asked whether the words "appointment or
nomination" in Section 87202 refer to the date of the Governor's
action or to the date on which the appointment or nomination
is confirmed by the Commission on Judicial Appointments.  We
interpret this phrase to mean the date of the Governor's
action, in keeping with the use of those terms in Article 6,
Section 16 of the California Constitution, which sets forth
the Governor's authority to fill vacancies in the courts by
appointment, and which provides that:

     A nomination or appointment by the Governor is effective
     when confirmed by the Commission on Judicial Appointments.

     This language implies that the appointment or nomination is
an action of the Governor, taken at some time before the
Commission's confirmation of it.  Indeed, one purpose in
requiring a statement of economic interests to be filed
within 10 days after appointment or nomination is to make
the statement available for review by the Commission on
Judicial Appointments during your deliberations.


1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise noted.

Honorable Rose Elizabeth Bird
March 12, 1980
Page Two


Your second question concerns the phrase "completes a
term of office" as used in Section 87205, which provides
that:

     A person who completes a term of an office specified
in Section 87200 and on the same day begins a term of
the same office or another such office of the same
jurisdiction is not deemed to assume office or leave
office.

     We interpret this phrase to mean the day the official
leaves office, regardless of whether he or she resigns the
post or serves the full statutory term. A person who resigns
from the bench of one California court to assume the bench
of another on the same day would not therefore have to file
either a leaving office or an assuming office statement, nor
would a person sitting on the bench of one California court
have to file an additional statement by virtue of being
appointed or nominated to sit on a different court. If the
Commission on Judicial Appointments is interested in seeing
a statement of economic interests filed by such a person, a
copy of his or her previous annual or assuming office statement,
as a public document, is available from the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

     If there is any further assistance this office could
offer you, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   /Sarah T. Cameron
                                   Deputy Chief for
                                   Conflicts of Interest
                                   Legal Division

STC:plh

Supreme Court of California
State Building
San Francisco
94102

Rose Elizabeth Bird
Chief Justice

February 21, 1980


Mr. Thomas K. Houston
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Houston:

I am writing to you in my capacity as Chairperson of
the Commission on Judicial Appointments to inquire as
to the Fair Political Practices Commission's interpre-
tation of a portion of section 87202 of the Government
Code.

In pertinent part, that section reads as follows:
"Every person who is appointed or nominated to an
office specified in Section 87200 shall file . . . a
statement [disclosing his investments and his interests
in real property] not more than 10 days after assuming
office, provided, however, that a person appointed or
nominated to such an office who is subject to confirmation
by the Commission on Judicial Appointments . . . shall
file such a statement no more than 10 days after the
appointment or nomination." It would be helpful to the
Commission on Judicial Appointments to know whether the
words "appointment or nomination" as used in that
sentence refer to the date of the Governor's action or
to the date on which the appointment or nomination is
confirmed by the Commission on Judicial Appointments.

It would also be helpful to know whether the reference
to "complet[ing] a term of an office," as that phrase
is used in section 87205, refers to the date on which
the term itself ends or to the date on which the individual
vacates that office, regardless of whether or not an
unexpired portion of the term remains.

Mr. Thomas K. Houston
February 21, 1980
Page 2


Any assistance which you could give to the Commission on
Judicial Appointments concerning these points would be
appreciated.  Thank you for your consideration of this
inquiry.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Rose Elizabeth Bird
Chief Justice
